title: Jupunba campestris

The Malpighiaceae family is native to the Americas and is characterized by its woody vines and shrubs. Jupunba campestris is likely part of a larger genus within the Malpighiaceae family, although specific details about its classification are not readily available. Further research may be necessary to determine its exact classification.
uses: Jupunba campestris has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in the treatment of various skin conditions and wounds due to its antimicrobial properties. It has been reported to exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities, which help in healing and preventing infections. In some cultures, Jupunba campestris is also used to treat gastrointestinal issues and as a natural remedy for diarrhea. Furthermore, its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain.
